qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting the RPP Annual Progress Report, and what are the consequences if it is not received by this date?
qa_pairs.answer: The deadline for the RPP Annual Progress Report is November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time, and extensions are not possible. If the annual report for a project is not received by this deadline, the Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) allocation for the current year will not be renewed.

qa_pairs.question: For multi-year Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) projects, what is the purpose of the mandatory annual progress report, and what kind of information is requested in the online form?
qa_pairs.answer: The mandatory RPP Annual Progress Report is required for multi-year projects to ensure that the limited computational resources offered by the Federation are being used effectively and efficiently. It also provides an opportunity to identify any issues that the Federation's support staff may be able to help alleviate. Information requested in the online form includes the operational status of the platform or portal, any ongoing issues, research outcomes (publications, presentations), significant changes in resource projections, detailed usage statistics for Canadian and international users, and training and outreach data for Highly Qualified Personnel (HQP). The reporting period for this information is August 8 to October 8, 2025.
